 {"id":"bd-m5wf.3.5","title":"Verification Gate: safe-merge correctness under high contention","description":"## Purpose

Verify safe-merge and adaptive conflict arbitration produce correct results under high contention.

## Scope

1. All merge proof variants produce correct merged state
2. Stress test: 100+ concurrent writers, mix of mergeable and unmergeable conflicts
3. Expected-loss of safe-merge is lower than strict FCW under measured contention
4. No data corruption in any scenario
5. All structured logging markers correct
6. All E2E scripts pass

## Acceptance criteria
- Stress test with 100+ concurrent writers passes
- Zero data corruption in any test
- Expected-loss comparison documented
- All sub-bead E2E scripts pass
- All sub-bead unit tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:12.847721733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T17:07:08.692001Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T17:07:08.691665Z","close_reason":"Verification gate passed: 120 writers across AppendOnly/IndependentKeys/DisjointBlocks/Strict scenarios. Zero data corruption. SafeMerge expected-loss (0.021) ~9.5x lower than Strict (0.198). Contention metrics tracked correctly (103 commits, 79 conflicts, 78 merges, 1 abort).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","mvcc","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:12.847721733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:08.265252218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:09.408231854Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4","title":"EPIC: Adaptive Repair Symbol Refresh","description":"## Purpose

Implement block-count staleness trigger for repair symbol refresh, beyond the age-only timeout (V1.1 OQ3: bounded staleness with lazy 30s default). Deferred from OQ3 because it requires explicit expected-loss improvement over age-only timeout.

## Scope

### Sub-beads

1. **Design: staleness-triggered refresh model** — Formal expected-loss comparison: age-only timeout vs block-count staleness trigger vs hybrid (age + block-count). Define the model, thresholds, and decision boundary.

2. **Implement block-count staleness tracking** — Track write count per repair group since last symbol refresh. Trigger eager refresh when block-count exceeds threshold.

3. **Implement hybrid refresh policy** — Combine age-timeout and block-count triggers. Configurable weights and thresholds. Runtime-selectable policy.

4. **SLO instrumentation** — Stale-window percentile tracking (p50/p95/p99 time-since-last-refresh). Alert when stale window exceeds SLO.

5. **Verification gate** — Expected-loss improvement proven, SLO met, E2E validation.

## Non-goals
- Changing the repair symbol encoding format
- Multi-host symbol exchange (separate epic)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:55.088476044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:55.088476044Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","durability","reliability","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:55.088476044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.1","title":"Design expected-loss model for block-count vs age-only symbol refresh","description":"## Purpose

Formal expected-loss comparison of refresh policies: age-only timeout, block-count staleness, and hybrid.

## Scope

1. **Loss model** — Define:
   - P(data_loss | stale_symbols) as function of blocks-written-since-refresh
   - P(data_loss | stale_symbols) as function of time-since-refresh
   - Cost of unnecessary refresh (I/O overhead)
   - Expected loss = P(loss) × severity + P(unnecessary_refresh) × cost

2. **Policy comparison** — For realistic workload profiles (idle, light writes, heavy writes, burst):
   - Age-only (current 30s default)
   - Block-count (refresh after N writes to group)
   - Hybrid (min of age-timeout and block-count)

3. **Decision boundary** — At what write rate does block-count trigger dominate age-only? Visualize as expected-loss curves.

4. **Design document** — docs/design-adaptive-refresh.md

## Acceptance criteria
- Expected-loss model with explicit probability and cost assignments
- Policy comparison for 4 workload profiles
- Decision boundary identified
- Design document with analysis
- Unit tests for expected-loss calculation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:56.072811428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T20:53:07.298586Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T20:53:07.298188Z","close_reason":"Implemented RefreshLossModel with expected-loss comparison of age-only vs block-count vs hybrid refresh policies. Design doc at docs/design-adaptive-refresh.md. Key findings: refresh I/O cost dominates under default parameters; block-count triggers become optimal when data_loss_cost >> refresh_io_cost/(crash_rate*corruption_prob). 10 unit tests pass, full workspace clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bayesian","design","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:56.072811428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","title":"Implement block-count staleness tracking per repair group","description":"## Purpose

Track the number of block writes per repair group since last symbol refresh. Trigger eager refresh when threshold exceeded.

## Scope

1. **Write counter** — Per-group atomic counter in RepairGroupStorage. Incremented on every committed block write to the group. Reset on symbol refresh.

2. **Threshold configuration** — Configurable block-count threshold (default: 1000 blocks). When counter exceeds threshold, schedule eager symbol refresh.

3. **Eager refresh trigger** — Hook into the repair pipeline to trigger immediate symbol refresh for groups exceeding threshold.

4. **Integration** — Wire write counter into ffs-mvcc commit path (post-commit hook increments group counters).

## Acceptance criteria
- Per-group write counter tracked
- Threshold-based eager refresh triggers correctly
- Counter reset after refresh
- Unit tests: counter increment, threshold trigger, reset
- E2E script: write N blocks, verify refresh triggered
- Structured logging for threshold events","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:57.045830254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T20:53:36.702023Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:57.045830254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:10.489391605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","title":"Implement block-count staleness tracking per repair group","description":"## Purpose

Track the number of block writes per repair group since last symbol refresh. Trigger eager refresh when threshold exceeded.

## Scope

1. **Write counter** — Per-group atomic counter in RepairGroupStorage. Incremented on every committed block write to the group. Reset on symbol refresh.

2. **Threshold configuration** — Configurable block-count threshold (default: 1000 blocks). When counter exceeds threshold, schedule eager symbol refresh.

3. **Eager refresh trigger** — Hook into the repair pipeline to trigger immediate symbol refresh for groups exceeding threshold.

4. **Integration** — Wire write counter into ffs-mvcc commit path (post-commit hook increments group counters).

## Acceptance criteria
- Per-group write counter tracked
- Threshold-based eager refresh triggers correctly
- Counter reset after refresh
- Unit tests: counter increment, threshold trigger, reset
- E2E script: write N blocks, verify refresh triggered
- Structured logging for threshold events","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:57.045830254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T21:00:44.188801Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T21:00:44.185097Z","close_reason":"Implemented per-group write counter (writes_since_refresh) in GroupRefreshState, block_count_threshold configuration, BlockCountThreshold refresh mode, threshold-exceeded logging, counter reset on refresh, and public API (set_block_count_threshold, writes_since_refresh). All 334 ffs-repair tests pass, full workspace clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:57.045830254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:10.489391605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","title":"Implement hybrid refresh policy (age + block-count)","description":"## Purpose

Combine age-timeout and block-count triggers into a hybrid refresh policy. Runtime-configurable.

## Scope

1. **RefreshPolicy enum** — `AgeOnly(Duration)`, `BlockCount(u64)`, `Hybrid { age: Duration, block_count: u64 }`.

2. **Hybrid logic** — Refresh triggers when EITHER age timeout OR block-count threshold is exceeded. This is the expected-loss-optimal policy.

3. **CLI configuration** — `--refresh-policy age-only|block-count|hybrid` with threshold parameters.

4. **Runtime switching** — Policy can be changed without restart via signal or evidence command.

## Acceptance criteria
- RefreshPolicy enum with 3 variants
- Hybrid triggers on either condition
- CLI flags for policy selection
- Unit tests for each policy variant
- E2E script testing policy switching","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.000469060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:11.599772178Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.000469060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:11.598803512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","title":"Implement stale-window SLO instrumentation and alerting","description":"## Purpose

Track stale-window percentiles (time and block-count since last refresh per group) and alert when SLO is exceeded. This bead focuses on the SLO *policy* layer (thresholds, breach detection, alerting); raw repair metrics (scrub rate, decode success) are handled by bd-m5wf.8.4. Uses the metrics framework from bd-m5wf.8.1 for counter/gauge/histogram primitives.

## Scope

1. **Stale-window metrics** — Per-group tracking of:
   - Time since last symbol refresh (uses Gauge from bd-m5wf.8.1)
   - Blocks written since last refresh (uses Counter from bd-m5wf.8.1)
   - p50/p95/p99 across all groups (uses Histogram from bd-m5wf.8.1)

2. **SLO definition** — Configurable SLO: max acceptable stale window (default: p95 < 60s OR p95 < 5000 blocks). SLO configuration via RepairConfig struct.

3. **Alert emission** — Structured log marker `repair_stale_window_slo_breach` when any group exceeds SLO. Does NOT duplicate the raw metric emission from bd-m5wf.8.4; only emits policy-level breach events.

4. **Evidence integration** — `ffs evidence --preset repair-freshness` shows per-group staleness.

## Acceptance criteria
- Stale-window percentiles tracked via bd-m5wf.8.1 histogram primitives
- SLO breach detected and logged (policy-level, not raw metrics)
- Evidence preset for repair freshness
- Unit tests for SLO threshold logic and percentile-based breach detection
- E2E script validating SLO alerting fires when stale windows exceed threshold","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.945075625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T06:09:54.045128683Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","observability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:58.945075625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:12.691667492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T06:09:54.043823158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","title":"Verification Gate: adaptive refresh expected-loss improvement","description":"## Purpose

Verify adaptive refresh has measurably lower expected loss than age-only timeout.

## Scope

1. Expected-loss comparison documented for 4 workload profiles
2. Hybrid policy reduces stale window p95 by >30% under write-heavy workloads
3. SLO instrumentation working correctly
4. No regression in repair correctness
5. All sub-bead unit tests and E2E scripts pass

## Acceptance criteria
- Expected-loss improvement >10% documented
- Stale-window p95 reduction >30% under heavy writes
- All sub-bead verification passes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:59.888098743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:13.787540241Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["durability","qa_e2e_required","qa_unit_required","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:22:59.888098743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-m5wf.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-m5wf.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-13T04:28:13.786640424Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
